frame_label: Misogyny Jokes: Childbearing/Rearing as Women's Responsibility
generated_comment:
Ramesh: Arre bhai, naukri apni jagah hai, par bachcha hone ke baad maa ko hi zyada dekhna padta hai.
Wife: Aaj kal dono sambhalte hain.
Ramesh: Haan, baap madad kar deta hai, lekin maa ki jagah kaun le sakta hai? Bachpan nikal gaya toh career se kya fayda.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covert misogyny because it presents childcare as naturally the mother's primary duty and frames the father as merely 'helping.' It encodes naturalizing language about maternal responsibility and career-sacrifice framing: the idea that a woman's career matters less once children are involved.
persona_text: You are Ramesh. Age 54. Married. A government school teacher in a small town in Madhya Pradesh. Your daughter Priya is 27 and works as a software engineer in Pune. She is doing well professionally and seems happy. But she is not thinking about marriage and when you bring it up she changes the subject. You are sitting with your wife after dinner. Priya has gone to bed. You are telling your wife what you think needs to happen and what you are going to do about it and what it will mean for Priya if she waits until she is 30 and what people in your town are already saying about a 27 year old unmarried working woman.
